description: HP 8757D Scalar Network Analyzer (also 8757C/8757E) for magnitude-only transmission and reflection measurements using external detectors (inputs A, B, C, R) on four independent measurement channels. Legacy HP-IB (IEEE 488-1978 / IEC-625) instrument; the interface does NOT support IEEE 488.2, so there is no '*IDN?'. The instrument is compatible with HP 8757A/C/E programming codes and is programmed with a two-letter mnemonic code language. Codes carrying a numeric value use a single-character variable convention: 'd' = variable-length numeric value, 'm' = 0 for off / 1 for on, 'n' = decimal integer 1 to 9, 'q' = a unique value, 's' = an ASCII or binary string. Numeric values returned by output commands may be transferred as ASCII characters or as 16-bit integers (most significant byte first); a single point or an entire 401-point (or 101/201/801/1601-point) trace may be transferred. Most parameters that carry a numeric value are read back with the Output Interrogated Parameter command 'OP' followed by the function prefix (e.g. 'OPRL' reads the reference level). HP-IB service interrupts (SRQs) are generated on front-panel key pressed, self-test error, sweep/plot/print operation complete, limit test fail, and illegal command. Footnote-restricted codes: superscript 1 = HP 8757D only; 2 = HP 8757D Option 001 only; 3 = HP 8757E firmware revision 3.1 or above; 4 = requires HP 8340/8341/8360 synthesized sweeper with the 8757 system interface connected and active; 5 = limit-line functions valid only for channels 1 or 2. Source: Table 2, 'Alphabetical Listing of HP 8757D/E Programming Codes', in the HP 8757D User's Guide / HP-IB Quick Reference Guide (08757-90130).

identity.description: Output identity. Returns the instrument model/identity string. The 8757D has no IEEE 488.2 '*IDN?' query; OI is the documented identity-output command.

commands.name: output_status_byte
commands.description: Output status bytes (status byte and extended status byte). The status byte reports HP-IB syntax error, operation (sweep/plot/print) complete, softkey pressed, change in extended status byte, numeric entry completed, and front-panel key pressed; the extended status byte reports detector uncal, front-panel preset/power-on, limit test failed, action requested, knob activity, operation not possible, and self-test failure.
